item.title: Capability snapshot files written without mode 0600 — credentials copies world-readable
item.intent: Capability snapshot files that contain credentials.json content must be written with mode 0600 — credential copies in snapshot dirs must never be world-readable.
item.description: src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts line 121 calls writeFileSync(destPath, content, "utf-8") without a mode option when copying files into snapshot directories. Snapshot targets include engine/credentials.json (for 'integrations' kind) and ops/integrations/.integration-manifest.json — meaning credentials are copied into snapshot files with default permissions (0644), making them world-readable. The directory structure is correctly created with DIR_MODE_SECRET (lines 82, 102, 113) but the files themselves are unprotected. Additionally, restoreCapabilitySnapshot at line 183 calls mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true }) without mode when restoring snapshot content to the deployment directory, potentially re-creating parent directories with 0755. Fix: add mode FILE_MODE_SECRET to writeFileSync at line 121, chmodSync after, and add DIR_MODE_SECRET to mkdirSync at line 183, chmodSync after.
steps: Read src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts lines 18-24 — confirm chmodSync is not in the node:fs import and FILE_MODE_SECRET is not in the defaults import
Line 18: add chmodSync to the node:fs import destructure — result: import { chmodSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
Line 24: add FILE_MODE_SECRET to the defaults import — change to: import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from '../../config/defaults.js';

Line 121 (in createCapabilitySnapshot, the else branch): change writeFileSync(destPath, content, 'utf-8') to writeFileSync(destPath, content, { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }); — this protects credential and manifest files copied into snapshot dirs
After line 121: add chmodSync(destPath, FILE_MODE_SECRET); — fixes permissions on existing installs
Line 183 (in restoreCapabilitySnapshot): change mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET });
After line 183: add chmodSync(destDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET); — fixes permissions when restore re-creates parent dirs
Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern='capability-snapshot' and confirm all tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot files copied from integration targets (engine/credentials.json, .integration-manifest.json) are written with mode 0600 — stat on any file under ops/rollback/integrations/snap-*/engine/credentials.json confirms no group/other read bits
Restore operation creates destination parent directories with mode 0700 — stat on dirs recreated during restore (e.g. ops/integrations/) confirms mode 0700
All existing capability-snapshot tests pass with no regressions
